name: United Neighborhood
description_short: Nonprofit community organization providing emergency financial assistance, job training, housing support, and social services to families and seniors in Northeastern Pennsylvania since 1923.
description_long:
United Neighborhood Centers of Northeastern Pennsylvania (UNC) is one of the region's largest and longest-operating nonprofit organizations, with roots dating back to 1923 when members of Bethel Church opened a settlement house to serve the African American community in Scranton. The organization expanded in 1924 to serve immigrant populations and formally merged in 1970 to become the unified entity it is today. UNC operates as a 501(c)(3) organization dedicated to meeting the needs of families facing financial challenges, seniors, youth, and new immigrants.

UNC offers a comprehensive wrap-around approach across five programmatic departments: Community Services, Children and Teens, Community Education, Community Revitalization, and Community Health and Aging, plus a Housing/Community Development Corporation. Their services include emergency financial assistance for rent, housing, food, and utilities; job readiness and workforce development programs; child care services; youth and teen programming; housing support; services for older adults; and community revitalization initiatives. The organization also runs farmers markets and educational programs like PROSPER (for parents and 5th-8th grade children) and SCOLA.

UNC distinguishes itself through its deep community roots spanning over 100 years, its settlement house model focused on neighborhood-level problem solving, and its holistic approach to addressing multiple needs simultaneously. The organization maintains partnerships with United Way of Lackawanna & Wayne Counties, Alliance for Strong Families and Communities, Housing Alliance of Pennsylvania, and other regional coalitions. Their board includes professionals from healthcare, education, and finance sectors, suggesting strategic community leadership.

UNC is appropriate for Northeastern Pennsylvania residents needing immediate emergency assistance or comprehensive social services. However, the website provides limited detail about eligibility criteria, income thresholds, or specific program availability. Those seeking specialized credit counseling should verify if UNC offers HUD-approved or NFCC-certified credit counseling services, as the website emphasizes emergency assistance and broader community development rather than credit-specific interventions.
services: Job readiness and workforce development programs
pros: Long-established nonprofit with 100+ years operating in Northeastern Pennsylvania community
Multiple funding sources including $25,000 PNC commitment and $10,000 Hawk Family Foundation grants
Comprehensive emergency assistance for rent, housing, food, and utilities via dedicated Community Service Department
501(c)(3) status means donations are tax-exempt and organization complies with all nonprofit regulations
Wrap-around service model addressing multiple needs simultaneously across five programmatic departments
Operates Housing/Community Development Corporation for dedicated housing support
Member of multiple regional alliances including United Way and Housing Alliance of Pennsylvania
cons: Website lacks clear eligibility requirements or income thresholds for assistance programs
No specific information about whether organization offers HUD-approved or NFCC-certified credit counseling
Limited detail on application process, documentation requirements, or typical approval timelines
Website emphasizes emergency assistance but provides minimal specifics on program scope, funding availability, or maximum assistance amounts
No online application or intake system mentioned; assistance appears to require phone contact only
best_for: Northeastern Pennsylvania residents facing immediate emergencies with rent, utilities, food, or housing
Families seeking integrated services combining emergency assistance with job training and education
Low-income seniors and new immigrants needing comprehensive community support services
Parents and caregivers looking for youth programming and family strengthening initiatives
diagnosis: UNC is best for Northeastern Pennsylvania residents facing immediate financial emergencies or those needing comprehensive, integrated social services spanning emergency assistance, job training, housing, and family support. The main caveat is that the website provides limited transparency about eligibility criteria, assistance amounts, and whether they offer specialized credit counseling—callers should contact the Community Service Department at 570.343.8835 to verify specific program availability and requirements.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_description: Nonprofit providing emergency financial assistance, job training, housing support, and social services in Northeastern Pennsylvania.
